data.narrative:
Starfish Space is a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Austin Link and Trevor Bennett that has raised $100M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-stage companies in the same sector, including Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $100M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $190M. Peer-median anchoring draws on three same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.3B and a range of $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable; while the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 64.2x price-to-sales across six listed companies, Starfish Space's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $162M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $745M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty: the stage-progression method anchors conservatively to disclosed capital, while peer comparables suggest substantially higher values depending on operational traction and technology differentiation relative to Cowboy, Aalyria, and Hubble. Resolution of this range hinges on clarity around revenue trajectory, unit economics, and technical progress in Starfish's core capability relative to the peer cohort.
